name: mysqlclient (MySQL-Python C Extension Driver)

performance.notes: C extension (libmysqlclient); fastest Python MySQL driver for synchronous code

agent_readiness.notes: DBAPI2 MySQL driver using C extension. import MySQLdb. conn = MySQLdb.connect(host='localhost', user='root', passwd='pass', db='mydb', charset='utf8mb4'). cursor = conn.cursor(MySQLdb.cursors.DictCursor). cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = %s', (1,)). rows = cursor.fetchall(). conn.commit(). cursor.executemany('INSERT INTO t (a,b) VALUES (%s,%s)', data). conn.close(). Same API as PyMySQL but C-based (faster). Used by Django as recommended MySQL backend (install_requires mysqlclient). Requires libmysqlclient-dev system package. No async — use aiomysql for async.

gotchas: Requires libmysqlclient-dev system package (apt install default-libmysqlclient-dev)
GPL-2.0 license — may affect commercial distribution
No async support — use aiomysql for async
Package name is mysqlclient but import is MySQLdb
MariaDB compatibility: works but some features differ
best_when: Django applications with MySQL (Django's recommended MySQL driver)
Synchronous Python apps needing fastest MySQL performance
Production MySQL workloads where PyMySQL is too slow
avoid_when: Environments where libmysqlclient-dev can't be installed (use PyMySQL)
When GPL license is an issue for your project
summary: mysqlclient is the fastest Python MySQL driver (C extension). import MySQLdb; conn = MySQLdb.connect(host='', user='', passwd='', db=''); cursor = conn.cursor(MySQLdb.cursors.DictCursor); cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM t WHERE id=%s', (id,)); rows = cursor.fetchall(). Django's recommended MySQL backend. GPL-2.0. Requires libmysqlclient-dev. No async — use aiomysql for async.
